description: Production-grade story decomposition for Authentication epic — foundation through enhancement tiers

stories.title: Registration Form & Email Validation
stories.user_story: As a new user, I want to register with my email and password, so that I can create an account and start tracking expenses.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the registration screen, When I enter a valid email and password meeting requirements (min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number), Then my account is created and a welcome email is sent
Given I enter an email already registered, When I tap 'Register', Then I see 'Account already exists' with a link to the login screen
Given I enter a password that doesn't meet requirements, When I tap 'Register', Then inline validation shows which requirements are not met
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 6 — consent for account creation

metadata.reasoning: Foundation story for all auth — no dependencies. 5 points: form UI + validation + email send + duplicate handling. Confidential: stores email/password.

trajectory.detail: Feature specifies email/password registration with validation rules
Foundation — all other auth stories depend on account existing
Registration form with full validation and error handling

stories.title: Login with Email/Password
stories.user_story: As a registered user, I want to log in with my email and password, so that I can access my expense data securely.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the login screen, When I enter valid credentials, Then I am authenticated and redirected to the home screen
Given I enter incorrect credentials, When I tap 'Login', Then I see 'Invalid email or password' without revealing which is wrong
Given I fail login 5 times, When I attempt a 6th login, Then my account is temporarily locked for 15 minutes with a message explaining why

metadata.reasoning: Core auth flow — depends on registration (US-001). 3 points: simpler than registration (no email send, no account creation). Includes rate limiting.

trajectory.detail: Depends on registration — can't login without account
Login flow with security (rate limiting, no credential enumeration)

stories.title: Password Reset Flow
stories.user_story: As a user who forgot my password, I want to reset it via email, so that I can regain access to my account.
acceptance_criteria: Given I tap 'Forgot Password', When I enter my registered email, Then a password reset link is sent (valid for 1 hour, single-use)
Given I click the reset link, When I enter a new password meeting requirements, Then my password is updated and all existing sessions are invalidated
Given I enter an unregistered email, When I tap 'Send Reset Link', Then the same success message is shown (no account enumeration)

metadata.reasoning: Enhancement to registration — not explicitly in feature description but essential for production auth. Lower confidence (inferred). 3 points: reset email + token + session invalidation.

trajectory.detail: Password reset is implied by email/password auth — users forget passwords
Reset flow with security best practices (no enumeration, expiry, single-use)

stories.title: Biometric Enrolment After First Login
stories.user_story: As a returning user, I want to enrol my biometric (Face ID or fingerprint) after my first successful login, so that I can log in faster next time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have just logged in with email/password for the first time, When the home screen loads, Then I am prompted 'Enable Face ID/fingerprint for faster login?'
Given I accept biometric enrolment, When the OS biometric prompt completes successfully, Then biometric login is enabled and confirmed
Given my device does not support biometrics, When login completes, Then no biometric prompt is shown and password-only flow continues

metadata.reasoning: Foundation for biometric feature — enrolment must happen before biometric login works. Depends on password login (US-002). 5 points: OS biometric API + secure storage + device capability check. Restricted: biometric data.

trajectory.detail: Feature specifies enrolment after first successful login
Must happen before biometric login — prerequisite
Enrolment prompt with device capability detection

stories.title: Biometric Login for Returning Users
stories.user_story: As a returning user with biometrics enabled, I want to log in using Face ID or fingerprint, so that I can access my data without typing my password.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have biometrics enabled, When I open the app, Then the OS biometric prompt appears automatically
Given biometric authentication succeeds, When the OS confirms identity, Then I am logged in and see the home screen within 2 seconds
Given biometric fails 3 consecutive times, When I attempt a 4th time, Then biometric is disabled for this session and I am prompted to enter my password

metadata.reasoning: Core biometric flow — depends on enrolment (US-004). 5 points: OS biometric API + session management + fallback logic. Restricted: biometric verification.

trajectory.detail: Feature specifies Face ID/fingerprint login for returning users
Depends on enrolment — can't use biometric without setting it up
Biometric login with 3-attempt fallback to password

stories.title: Biometric Revocation and Re-enrolment
stories.user_story: As a user, I want to disable biometric login from settings and optionally re-enrol, so that I have control over my authentication method.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am in Settings > Security, When I tap 'Disable Biometric Login', Then biometric is disabled immediately and I must use password for next login
Given biometrics are disabled, When I tap 'Enable Biometric Login', Then the enrolment flow is triggered again
Given I have changed my device's registered biometrics (e.g., added new fingerprint), When I next open the app, Then I am prompted to re-authenticate with password before biometric is re-enabled

metadata.reasoning: Enhancement — management of biometric settings. Lower confidence (inferred from 'optional biometric' implying user control). 3 points: settings UI + revocation + re-enrolment. Restricted: modifies biometric config.

trajectory.detail: Settings-based management with security re-check on biometric change

content.execution_summary:
• Produced 6 stories (24 points total) for 1 epic, 2 features
• Feature coverage: F-05.1 (3 stories, 11 pts), F-05.2 (3 stories, 13 pts)
• Dependency chain: US-001 → US-002 → US-004 → US-005 → US-006; US-001 → US-003
• Key decisions: added password reset (US-003) as inferred necessity — confidence 0.88. Added biometric revocation (US-006) as user control requirement.
• Reflection: verified no circular dependencies, all ACs are Given/When/Then, no story exceeds 5 points. US-003 and US-006 are inferred (not explicit in feature) — flagged with lower confidence.
• Data sensitivity: 3 stories Confidential (email/password), 3 stories Restricted (biometric).
